subject: Anthropology
title: Formalism vs. Substantivism
description: A 60-second lesson on Formalism vs. Substantivism.

hook.text: Can supply and demand explain a potlatch feast? Do tribal economies follow the same rules as Wall Street?

buildup.text: Formalists say yes: all humans maximize utility. Economic models apply everywhere, from markets to villages.

discovery.text: Substantivists say no: economies are embedded in social relations. You can't separate economics from culture.

twist.text: Karl Polanyi argued that the self-regulating market is a modern invention, not a natural state of human affairs.

climax.text: The debate shaped the field. Today most agree that economies are always cultural, even when they look purely rational.

punchline.text: Markets are cultural creations, not natural laws.

quiz.question: What do substantivists argue about economies?
options: Economies are embedded in social and cultural relations
